sourceTitle: How startups conduct user research using Notion

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of adding custom properties to a Notion table database for user research?

options.text: It automatically generates interview transcripts.
options.explanation: Custom properties store metadata; they don’t create transcripts.

options.text: It lets you capture specific details like research type, date, and interviewee information.
options.explanation: Custom properties are designed to hold exactly those pieces of information.

options.text: It changes the visual theme of the database.
options.explanation: Visual themes are controlled elsewhere; properties affect data, not appearance.

options.text: It enables real‑time video conferencing within the table.
options.explanation: Video conferencing isn’t a function of custom properties.

questions.question: How can you turn a regular table entry into a dedicated space for an interview’s full details?

options.text: By converting the entry into a page.
options.explanation: Each table entry is a page where you can store all interview information.

options.text: By attaching a PDF file to the row.
options.explanation: Attachments are possible but don’t create a full page for detailed notes.

options.text: By linking the row to an external Google Doc.
options.explanation: External links are useful but don’t turn the entry itself into a Notion page.

options.text: By enabling the ‘quick view’ mode.
options.explanation: Quick view only shows a preview; it doesn’t provide a full workspace.

questions.question: Why might a startup create separate templates for different research types in Notion?

options.text: To ensure each research type follows a consistent structure and saves time when logging new entries.
options.explanation: Templates standardize the process and speed up data entry.

options.text: To automatically publish research findings to social media.
options.explanation: Templates don’t include publishing capabilities.

options.text: To lock the database so only managers can edit it.
options.explanation: Templates affect layout, not permissions.

options.text: To convert the research data into a spreadsheet format.
options.explanation: Templates stay within Notion; they don’t export to spreadsheets automatically.

questions.question: What is the purpose of creating a Relation between the user research database and meeting notes?

options.text: It allows you to view meeting notes directly within each research entry, linking context together.
options.explanation: Relations connect related records, giving quick access to associated meeting notes.

options.text: It merges both databases into a single table, deleting the original separate tables.
options.explanation: Relations link tables; they don’t merge or delete them.

options.text: It automatically schedules follow‑up meetings based on research data.
options.explanation: Scheduling isn’t a built‑in function of Relations.

options.text: It encrypts the research data for added security.
options.explanation: Relations are about data linkage, not encryption.

questions.question: If a startup wants to see only interviews conducted in the last month, which Notion feature should they use?

options.text: Create a new view with a filter on the date property for the last 30 days.
options.explanation: Filters let you limit records by criteria such as date.

options.text: Add a table of contents at the top of each page.
options.explanation: A table of contents helps navigation, not filtering.

options.text: Enable the ‘auto‑sort’ toggle on the database.
options.explanation: Sorting changes order but doesn’t limit which records appear.

options.text: Use the template gallery to download a pre‑made filter.
options.explanation: Templates provide structure, not dynamic filters.

questions.question: Which step is necessary before you can add a new template for a specific research type?

options.text: Click the downwards arrow at the top right of the table and select “New template”.
options.explanation: The article specifies this exact sequence to create a new template.

options.text: Duplicate an existing page and rename it.
options.explanation: Duplicating a page creates a copy, not a reusable template.

options.text: Enable “Template Mode” in the workspace settings.
options.explanation: There is no such setting mentioned in the guide.

options.text: Import a CSV file containing template data.
options.explanation: CSV import is for data, not for creating Notion templates.

questions.question: How does adding a Table of Contents (TOC) at the top of each interview page improve the research workflow?

options.text: It provides quick navigation to different sections within a long interview page, making information retrieval faster.
options.explanation: A TOC lists headings, allowing users to jump directly to relevant parts.

options.text: It automatically summarizes the interview content into bullet points.
options.explanation: A TOC lists headings; it doesn’t generate summaries.

options.text: It syncs the interview page with external project management tools.
options.explanation: Syncing is not a function of a TOC.

options.text: It locks the page so only the original author can edit it.
options.explanation: TOC has no impact on permissions.
